title: Hi-C library construction from young Maize leaves
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8vma0","text":"In a recent study we constructed a high-quality chromosome-level reference genome for the maize cultivar Dan340 by combining PacBio long HiFi sequencing reads, Illumina short reads and chromosomal conformational capture (Hi-C) sequencing reads. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"65qpc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4n8o0","text":"A Hi-C library was constructed using young leaves following previously published procedures with slight modifications. See the source paper and the following protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ddfl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a02ul","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1i0na","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"guid":"496220B4341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"Hi-C: a comprehensive technique to capture the conformation of genomes.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"Methods (San Diego, Calif.)","date":"2012-11-01T00:00:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ymeth.2012.05.001","authors":"Belton JM, McCord RP, Gibcus JH, Naumova N, Zhan Y, Dekker J","can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Approximately 5 g leaf samples from seedling were cut into minute pieces and cross-linked by 4% formaldehyde solution at room temperature in a vacuum for 00:30:00 .
Each sample was mixed with an excess of 2.5 Molarity (M) glycine to quench the crosslinking reaction for 00:05:00 and then placed on ice for 00:15:00 .

The cross-linked DNA was extracted and then digested for 12:00:00 with 20 units of DpnII restriction enzyme (NEB) at 37 °C , and the resuspended mixture was incubated at 65 °C for 00:20:00 to inactivate the restriction enzyme.
The sticky ends of the digested fragments were biotinylated and proximity ligated to form enriched ligation junctions and then ultrasonically sheared to a size of 300 - 600 bp.
The biotin-labelled DNA fragments were pulled down and ligated with Illumina paired-end adapters, and then amplified by PCR to produce the Hi-C sequencing library.
The library was sequenced using an Illumina HiSeq X Ten platform with 2 × 150 bp paired-end reads (Illumina, San Diego, CA, USA).
After removing low-quality sequences and trimming adapter sequences, we had 304.37 Gb (approximately 130×) of clean data generated. This is then used for genome assembly.
creator: Hongling Zhou
